zh.lifeSummary: Edward Avery是John Avery的兒子，於1530年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特。他於1550年與Margaret Stevens在英國英格蘭戴文艾可謝特結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年12月7日在英國戴文托特斯享年96歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦可利普萊加特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Edward Avery是John Avery的儿子，于1530年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特。他于1550年与Margaret Stevens在英国英格兰戴文艾可謝特结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1626年12月7日在英国戴文托特斯享年96岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦可利普萊加特。

nameMeanings.name: Avery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Anglo-Norman French personal name Aevery, a Norman form of Alfred . Among the 17th-century Puritan settlers in New England, there was some confusion with Averill . Compare Averett .
Altered form of French Canadian Hévey (see Hevey ). Compare Avey 3.

History: Christopher Avery emigrated from England to Salem, MA, in or before 1630. — William Avery (alias Averill) was one of the Puritan settlers who emigrated from England to Ipswich, MA, in or c. 1637. — Some of the American bearers of the surname Avery are descendants of Nicolas Hévé/Devé from France, who was in QC by 1672 (see Hevey ).

variants.name: Avara
meanings: Altered form of English   Avery  . See also   Avera  .
Italian (Sicily): from the feminine form of Avaro, a nickname from avaro ‘avaricious, mean, miserly’. This surname is very rare in Italy.

variants.name: Averill
meanings: English:

apparently from the name of the month (April), Middle English averil, Old French avrill, Latin aprīlis, perhaps with reference to freshness of temperament, or youth, or possibly to the changeable weather of the month.

perhaps occasionally a habitational name from Haverhill in Suffolk, with loss of initial H-. The placename derives from either Old English hæfera ‘oats’ or hæfer ‘he-goat’ + hyll ‘hill’. Compare Avery .

variants.name: Avera
meanings: Altered form of English   Avery  .

History: Richard Avery, from Fillongley, Warwickshire, is recorded in Surry, VA, in 1672. The surname was changed to Avera in the following generation and later also to Avara. A Jacob Avera is recorded in Johnson County, NC in 1764.

variants.name: Avary
meanings: English: variant of   Avery  .

variants.name: Every
meanings: English (southwestern): variant of   Avery  .

variants.name: Alfred
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Arianne, Armand, Camille, Germine, Jacques, Jean-Claude, Magalie, Marcel, Michel, Patrice.

English, French, German, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the Middle English personal name Alfred, Elfred, Old English Ælfrǣd ‘elf counsel’. This owed its popularity as a personal name in England to the fame of the West Saxon king Alfred the Great (849–899), who defeated the Danes, keeping them out of Wessex, and whose court was a great center of learning and culture.

variants.name: Hevey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Nicolle.

French Canadian (Hévey): altered form of French Hévé, itself a variant or an altered form of Devé, an unflattering nickname from desvé ‘mad, insane’. The surname Devé originates from Normandy. Compare Avery 2 and Avey 3.
Irish (Meath): variant of   Heaphy  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Edward Avery was born in 1530, in Exeter, Devon, England as the son of John Avery. He married Margaret Stevens in 1550, in Exeter, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 7 December 1626, in Totnes, Devon, England, at the age of 96, and was buried in Cripplegate, London, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Averyは1530年にイギリスデボンエクセターでJohn Averyの息子として生まれた。1550年にイギリスイギリスデボンエクセターでMargaret Stevensと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1626年12月7日にイギリスデボントトンスで96歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンクリップレガトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Avery(은)는 1530년 영국 데폰 세터에서 John Avery의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 세터에서 Margaret Stevens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 12월 7일 영국 데폰 토트네스에서 96세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크리플레가트에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Edward Avery родился в 1530 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. Его отца звали John Avery. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Stevens в 1550 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 7 декабря 1626 г. в местности тотнесь, девон, Англия в возрасте 96 лет и был похоронен в местности крипплегэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
